It seems like you're trying to force-push your changes directly from your local branch to the main branch without checking for conflicts or updates on the remote repository first. I would advise against this approach, as it can lead to lost work and version control issues.

Instead, let's try a safer approach: we can use `git pull` with the `--rebase` option to update our local branch with any changes from the main branch, resolve any conflicts, and then force-push the updated local branch. This way, we ensure that our changes are on top of the latest remote updates.

Here's an example command you can try: `git pull origin main --rebase && git push origin <your-local-branch-name> -f`.
